Kendall Square urban renewal : environmental impact statement.

The Kendall Square Urban Renewal Project in Cambridge, Massachusetts, aimed to revitalize a largely cleared area by introducing light industry, office spaces, retail stores, and possibly a hotel and residential units. The project was intended to eliminate blight, boost the local economy, and improve public utilities. However, it also posed challenges such as increased traffic and noise, and the relocation of six businesses. The project was a continuation of an urban renewal effort initially centered around NASA, which had since withdrawn from the area.
Department of Housing and Urban Development

The Kendall Square Urban Renewal Project was proposed to revitalize a previously cleared area in Cambridge, Massachusetts, with the goal of eliminating urban blight and enhancing the local economy. While the project aimed to bring in new businesses and improve public utilities, it faced public pushback due to concerns about increased traffic, noise, and the displacement of existing businesses. Ultimately, the project moved forward as part of a broader urban renewal strategy, although it had to navigate various community concerns along the way.
United States. Department of Housing and Urban Development
